Operation 'getworkexpression' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Current description mentions format support but lacks clarity on what data is returned in each format, authentication requirements, and the structured nature of the FRBR URI parameters. Enhanced description specifies JSON includes metadata, clarifies authentication, and better explains the parameter structure for AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Retrieve a specific legislative work expression by its FRBR URI. Supports JSON, HTML, PDF, ePub, and zip formats.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a specific legislative work expression by its FRBR URI components (country, document type, date, number). Supports multiple output formats: JSON (with rich metadata including amendments and commencements), HTML, PDF, ePub, and zip. Returns the work expression in the requested format. Requires authentication via token.
data.reasoning: Current description mentions format support but lacks clarity on what data is returned in each format, authentication requirements, and the structured nature of the FRBR URI parameters. Enhanced description specifies JSON includes metadata, clarifies authentication, and better explains the parameter structure for AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'getworkexpressionatdate' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context. Current description is unclear about the operation's purpose (handling temporal versions of legislation) and lacks context about what 'point-in-time' means in the legislative domain. Enhanced description explains the temporal aspect, clarifies the @ syntax, specifies output formats, and adds authentication requirement for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Retrieve a specific expression of a work at a given date. Use @ for the first expression, @YYYY-MM-DD for an exact date.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a specific legislative work expression at a particular point in time, accounting for amendments and commencements. The expressionDate parameter accepts @ for the first/original expression or @YYYY-MM-DD format for a specific date. Returns the work expression as it existed on that date. Supports JSON, HTML, PDF, ePub, and zip formats. Requires authentication via token.
data.reasoning: Current description is unclear about the operation's purpose (handling temporal versions of legislation) and lacks context about what 'point-in-time' means in the legislative domain. Enhanced description explains the temporal aspect, clarifies the @ syntax, specifies output formats, and adds authentication requirement for better AI agent interpretability.
